NCT03349060.name: JADE MONO-1
NCT03349060.group: Moderate-severe AD monotherapy, abrocitinib 200 mg daily vs placebo (wk 12, IGA 0/1 RR)

allOutcomes.title: IGA 0/1 with >=2-grade improvement at week 12 (primary, abrocitinib 200 mg arm) (RR)

NCT03349060.snippet: Source: Simpson EL et al. Lancet 2020;396:255-266 (JADE MONO-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 387 adolescents and adults (age >=12) with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (IGA >=3, EASI >=16, BSA >=10%, inadequate response to topicals) randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to abrocitinib 200 mg (n=156), abrocitinib 100 mg (n=156), or matched placebo (n=77) orally once daily for 12 weeks as monotherapy (topical corticosteroids and calcineurin inhibitors discontinued). This app analyses the 200 mg vs placebo comparison (n=155 evaluable vs n=77).

evidence.label: Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 12
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 (clear/almost clear with >=2-grade improvement) at week 12: abrocitinib 200 mg 43.8% (67/155) vs placebo 7.9% (6/77); risk ratio 5.54 (95% CI 2.52-12.18; P<0.001). Co-primary EASI-75: 62.7% vs 11.8%. Both primary endpoints met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Pruritus, Quality of Life, Safety
evidence.text: PP-NRS >=4-point improvement at wk 12: 57% abrocitinib 200 vs 15% placebo. Nausea 14% (200 mg), headache 10%, herpes zoster rare. Cytopenias transient. Dose-dependent lipid changes. Thrombosis signal emerged in later pooled safety (tofacitinib-class boxed warning applies).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 91% completion. D4 LOW - central IGA rater training. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* 12 years of age or older with a minimum body weight of 40 kg~* Diagnosis of atopic dermatitis (AD) for at least 1 year and current status of moderate to severe disease (>= the following scores: BSA 10%, IGA 3, EASI 16, Pruritus NRS 4)~* Recent history of inadequate response or inability to tolerate topical AD treatments or require systemic treatments for AD control~Exclusion Criteria:~* Unwilling to discontinue current AD medications prior to the study or require treatmen...

NCT03575871.name: JADE MONO-2
NCT03575871.group: Moderate-severe AD monotherapy, abrocitinib 200 mg daily vs placebo (wk 12, IGA 0/1 RR)

allOutcomes.title: IGA 0/1 with >=2-grade improvement at week 12 (primary, abrocitinib 200 mg arm) (RR)

NCT03575871.snippet: Source: Silverberg JI et al. JAMA Dermatol 2020;156:863-873 (JADE MONO-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 391 adolescents and adults with moderate-to-severe AD randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to abrocitinib 200 mg, 100 mg, or placebo daily for 12 weeks as monotherapy. This app analyses the 200 mg vs placebo comparison (n=155 vs n=78).

evidence.label: Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 12
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 at week 12: abrocitinib 200 mg 38.1% (59/155) vs placebo 9.1% (7/78); risk ratio 4.25 (95% CI 2.04-8.85; P<0.001). Co-primary EASI-75: 61.0% vs 10.4%. Both met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Pruritus, Safety
evidence.text: PP-NRS >=4-point improvement: 55% (200 mg) vs 12% (placebo). Nausea 14%, nasopharyngitis 14%, acne 5%. Thrombocytopaenia transient. No MACE or VTE within trial duration.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 89% completion. D4 LOW - central IGA. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* 12 years of age or older with a minimum body weight of 40 kg~* Diagnosis of atopic dermatitis (AD) for at least 1 year and current status of moderate to severe disease (>= the following scores: BSA 10%, IGA 3, EASI 16, Pruritus NRS severity 4)~* Recent history of inadequate response or inability to tolerate topical AD treatments or require systemic treatments for AD control~Exclusion Criteria:~* Unwilling to discontinue current AD medications prior to the study or require...

NCT03569293.name: MEASURE-UP 1
NCT03569293.group: Moderate-severe AD monotherapy, upadacitinib 30 mg daily vs placebo (wk 16, IGA 0/1 RR)

allOutcomes.title: IGA 0/1 with >=2-grade improvement at week 16 (primary, upadacitinib 30 mg arm) (RR)

NCT03569293.snippet: Source: Guttman-Yassky E et al. Lancet 2021;397:2151-2168 (MEASURE-UP 1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 847 adolescents and adults with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (EASI >=16, IGA >=3, BSA >=10%) randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to upadacitinib 15 mg (n=281), upadacitinib 30 mg (n=285), or placebo (n=281) oral once daily for 16 weeks as monotherapy. This app analyses the 30 mg vs placebo comparison (n=281 evaluable).

evidence.label: Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 16
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 at week 16: upadacitinib 30 mg 62% (171/281) vs placebo 8% (23/281); risk ratio 7.43 (95% CI 4.99-11.08; P<0.0001). Co-primary EASI-75: 80% vs 16%. Both met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Pruritus, Worst-Itch, Safety
evidence.text: Worst-itch NRS >=4-point improvement: 60% (30 mg) vs 12% (placebo). Serious infection 1.1% vs 0.7%. Acne 17% (30 mg). Herpes zoster 2%. CPK elevations transient. Thrombocytopaenia rare. Class-wide boxed warning for MACE/thrombosis/malignancy applies to ongoing safety monitoring.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 92% completion. D4 LOW - central IGA. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Body weight of ≥ 40 kg at Baseline Visit for participants between ≥ 12 and < 18 years of age~* Chronic atopic dermatitis (AD) with onset of symptoms at least 3 years before Baseline Visit and subject meets Hanifin and Rajka criteria.~* Active moderate to severe AD defined by:~  * Eczema Area and Severity Index (EASI) score ≥ 16 at the Screening and Baseline Visits;~ * Validated Investigator's Global Assessment (vIGA) score ≥ 3 at the Screening and Baseline Visits;~ * ≥ 10...

NCT03607422.name: MEASURE-UP 2
NCT03607422.group: Moderate-severe AD monotherapy, upadacitinib 30 mg daily vs placebo (wk 16, IGA 0/1 RR)

allOutcomes.title: IGA 0/1 with >=2-grade improvement at week 16 (primary, upadacitinib 30 mg arm) (RR)

NCT03607422.snippet: Source: Reich K et al. Lancet 2021;397:2169-2181 (MEASURE-UP 2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 836 adolescents and adults with moderate-to-severe AD randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to upadacitinib 15 mg (n=276), 30 mg (n=282), or placebo (n=278) daily for 16 weeks monotherapy. This app analyses the 30 mg vs placebo comparison (n=276 evaluable).

evidence.label: Primary - IGA 0/1 at Week 16
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 at week 16: upadacitinib 30 mg 52% (144/276) vs placebo 5% (13/278); risk ratio 11.16 (95% CI 6.49-19.18; P<0.0001). Co-primary EASI-75: 73% vs 13%. Both met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Itch, Safety
evidence.text: Worst-itch NRS >=4-point improvement: 60% (30 mg) vs 9% (placebo). Acne 13% (30 mg). Serious infection 2% vs 2%. No MACE/VTE within trial duration. Class-level boxed warning applies.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Dermatology | JAK Inhibitors in Atopic Dermatitis v1.1
